title: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
subhead: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.

title: Fighter jet shot down in Iran, purges within the top ranks: The US military is in turmoil
Thousands rally against racism in Paris suburb to defend mayor
What is Israel's military objective in Lebanon?
subhead: Israel continues to expand its operations, fueling fears of a new occupation of southern Lebanon. While the military's official mission is to completely disarm Hezbollah, its operational goal appears to be simply to weaken the militia-party and create a more or less extensive buffer zone along its border.

title: Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker drifts alarmingly in the Mediterranean
subhead: The Arctic-Metagaz, a sanctioned vessel carrying liquefied natural gas, has been drifting dangerously since Libyan authorities failed to tow it to safety.

title: Nan Goldin brings her personal elegy to the Grand Palais
subhead: In her uneven yet moving retrospective, the American photographer revives lost loved ones through slideshows and videos.

title: 'Literature and economics both claim to offer a representation of the world and possible scenarios'
subhead: The theme of debt recurs in many novels of the 19th century. Based on this observation, Alexandre Péraud, a literature professor, explains in an interview with Le Monde how the rise of the novel went hand in hand with the emergence of financial capitalism.

New Saint-Denis mayor seeks to turn racist insults against him into 'a foundation for building things'
subhead: Bally Bagayoko, the new hard-left mayor of the northern Paris suburb, filed a complaint for 'public insults' of a racist nature on Thursday, after having been targeted by racist insults on the CNews channel since being elected. He has called for a large anti-racist rally to be held in his city on Saturday.

title: Pesticides and cancer: For the first time, researchers find a connection at the national level
subhead: French researchers have developed a model to map pesticide exposure across Peru to reported cancer clusters. They demonstrate a 'robust spatial association' between these chemicals and increased cancer incidence rates in over 400 areas.

title: Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town
subhead: For the past three years in the village of Goudelin, someone has taken pleasure in harassing women living alone, often elderly, by pounding on their doors in the middle of the night. Around 30 victims have been identified. Speculation is rampant about the knocker's identity, and the victims are finally being heard.
